  • directive — A class enhancing python dictionaries »

The Directive class — Handling users’ requests¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

class directive.directive.Directive(semperpy.core.tools.dictionary)¶

Public methods¶

Directive(*args,**kargs):

Constructor for a Directive instance, just calls its parents (dict) constructor with the paramters.

Private methods¶

validate_language(directive_name,directive_reader):
Parameters:
  • directive_name (string) – name of the language definition (probably the name of the directive)
  • directive_reader (any callable object which returns the language definition from a single string parameter) – an object able to return a dictionary compliant with what is defined in class:: LanguageDefinition. It receives the string directive_name. This means that the language definition can be stored in any format, or even be a python module.

This method gets the language definition for a directive through the directive_reader, deals recursively with inherited language definitions if specified. It checks the validity of the language definitions and builds a final language definition from inherited definitions.

Returns: the final language definition, the definition of how the language should describe the directive and the definition of how the keywords should be defined.

inherit_from(parent,child):
Parameters:
  • parent (dictionary) – the dictionary to inherit from
  • child (dictionary) – the dictionary to inherit into

Inserts into child any entry of parent which is not in child. From entries which are in child and in parent, inherit all the entries in the entry from parent into the child.

specialize_from(parent,child):
Parameters:
  • parent (dictionary) – the dictionary to override values from
  • child (dictionary) – the dictionary to ovveride into

Inserts into child any entry of parent which is not in child. Replaces in child any entry which is both in the parent and the child.

validate_language_part(semantic,language):
Parameters:
  • semantic (dictionary) – the definition of how the language should be defined
  • language (dictionary) – the language to check

Checks that the language definition contains all the required keywords and ensures that the different entries are of the correct type.

Returns the language after modification.

resolveSpecialKeywords(definition,directive,language):
Parameters:
  • definition (dictionary) – A dictionary written in the language definition under the keyword specialize_from.
  • directive (dictionary) – the directive given by the user.
  • language (dictionary) – the language being used to verify the directive

This method tries to extract keywords from the different expressions in definition which are defined in the language. It then applies the language definition to those keywords in order to make sure that, before evaluating the expressions, the keywords exist (possible default values) and are of the expected type.

In order to apply the language to a reduced set of keyword, this method creates a reduced directive and builds in memory a reduced language definition.

find_specials(definition,directive):
Parameters:
  • definition (dictionary) – A dictionary written in the language definition under the keyword specialize_from.
  • directive (dictionary) – the directive given by the user.

This methods evaluates expressions defined under the specialize_from in the language definition. The syntax is as follows:

...
specialize_from = {
    "value1 == 'hello' and value2 == 'world'": "hello",
    "value3 < 12": "not_twelve"
}
...

Where the keys are boolean expressions which can name any key in the user’s directive and the values are the name of directive definitions. The contents of the directive definition is loaded and its keys replace the current keys if they exist or they are added. If the evaluation of the expression if False, nothing is done.

This is useful to define the semantics of directive depending on values of some of its values.
